reasoning: The resolution criteria explicitly require 10 to 12 Starship launches to successfully reach space (62 miles altitude) *between January 1, 2025, and December 31, 2025*. As of the knowledge cutoff in November 2025, there have only been 5 Starship flights in 2025 (Flights 7 through 11). To reach 10 launches in the calendar year, SpaceX would have to launch 5 more times in less than two months, which contradicts their historical flight cadence (approximately one launch every 1.5 to 2.5 months). Furthermore, recent reporting indicates that Flight 12 will not occur until 2026 ("No More Launch this year"). The prediction market price on Polymarket was also 0.00 in late October. Given the physical impossibility of reaching 10 launches in 2025, the probability is at the lowest possible bound.
